operation: First, start on the XY workplane, draw a circle with radius 12 units, and extrude it 8 units along the positive Z axis to form the base cylinder. Next, select the top Z face of this base cylinder to set up a new workplane, sketch a regular 5-sided polygon with an 8-unit circumscribed diameter, then extrude the polygon 4 units upwards to form a concentric raised pentagonal boss. Finally, select the second highest Z face (the top face of the base cylinder surrounding the boss), create a workplane on it, and cut a 6-unit diameter through hole centered on this face through the full height of the base cylinder.
geometry: The model has a coaxial two-part structure: a larger circular cylinder forms the lower base, with a smaller regular pentagonal prism positioned concentrically on the base's top surface. A single central cylindrical through hole runs vertically along the shared axis of the two shapes, passing entirely through the lower cylindrical base directly beneath the pentagonal boss.
function: This part is designed to act as a control knob or fastener cap: the pentagonal boss enables torque application for turning or tightening the component, while the central through hole accommodates mounting onto a mating shaft or threaded fastener, with the cylindrical base providing a stable seating surface.
